title: Improvement Snapshot
purpose: Record the result of systematically improving a project. Score it across multiple dimensions, fix the highest-impact gap, and leave a trail so the next agent knows what was done and what to do next. The improvement history of a project is the sequence of these snapshots.
context: You are improving a project you may have never seen before. Read CLAUDE.md and .visionlog/vision.md to understand what it should be. Read the code to understand what it is. Score the gap. Fix the biggest one. Record everything. The next agent reads your snapshot before starting — your next_priority is their starting point.
constraints: Every score must cite specific evidence — a file, a function, a behavior. '5/10 feels about right' is not evidence.
Fix the highest-impact gap, not the easiest. A reliability bug that affects users outranks a documentation gap.
Never violate the project's own guardrails. Read .visionlog/guardrails/ before changing anything.
The next_priority field is mandatory and must explain why — the next agent needs to understand your reasoning, not just the label.
One improvement per run. Depth over breadth. Do one thing well.

project.description: Name of the project that was improved

scores.description: 1-10 per dimension. null means not applicable to this project type.

focus.description: Which dimension was improved in this run

improvement.description: What was fixed — one paragraph, specific enough that someone can verify it

delta.description: Before/after for each dimension that changed

next_priority.description: What the next improvement run should focus on AND why. This is the handoff to the next agent.
